ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — IIS w3wp.exe spawns shell/recon LOLBin on Exchange (China Chopper / ProxyLogon web-shell execution)
ucs.description: Detects the canonical Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM / Operation Exchange Marauder) post-exploitation pattern: the IIS worker process w3wp.exe spawning cmd.exe, PowerShell, or hands-on-keyboard recon/credential LOLBins. This is the smoking-gun signal that a China Chopper or ProxyLogon-class web shell dropped into an Exchange virtual directory is being interactively driven by the operator.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=w3wp.exe Processes.process_name IN (cmd.exe,powershell.exe,pwsh.exe,mshta.exe,cscript.exe,wscript.exe,net.exe,net1.exe,nltest.exe,whoami.exe,systeminfo.exe,tasklist.exe,ipconfig.exe,quser.exe,reg.exe,certutil.exe,bitsadmin.exe,procdump.exe,procdump64.exe,rundll32.exe,regsvr32.exe,sc.exe,wmic.exe,dsquery.exe,ldifde.exe,curl.exe,7z.exe,7za.exe,makecab.exe) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_id
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
| `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| search parent_process="*MSExchange*" OR process="*procdump*" OR process="*-ma*lsass*" OR process="*ntds*" OR process="*nltest*" OR process="*whoami*" OR process="*net*group*Domain*Admins*" OR process="*ekrn*" OR process="*Microsoft.Exchange.SystemMailbox*"
| eval risk_object=dest, risk_object_type="system"
| table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process process_name process
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — w3wp.exe child process indicative of web-shell driven post-ex on Exchange
let _exchange_ctx = dynamic(["MSExchange","OWA","ECP","EWS","ActiveSync","OAB","RPC","Mapi","MailboxReplicationProxyAppPool"]);
let _exec_lolbins = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe",
    "net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe",
    "ipconfig.exe","quser.exe","reg.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe",
    "procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","sc.exe",
    "wmic.exe","dsquery.exe","ldifde.exe","curl.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","makecab.exe",
    "esentutl.exe","vssadmin.exe","ntdsutil.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where FileName in~ (_exec_lolbins)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"            // skip pure machine-account context
     or FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","ntdsutil.exe","vssadmin.exe")  // keep if cred-theft tooling
| extend ExchangeContext = iff(
    InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (_exchange_ctx)
      or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\Exchange Server\"
      or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\inetsrv\",
    true, false)
| extend SilkTyphoonRecon = iff(
    ProcessCommandLine has_any (
      "whoami /all","net group \"Domain Admins\"","net group \"Exchange",
      "nltest /dclist","nltest /domain_trusts",
      "Get-MailboxExportRequest","New-MailboxExportRequest",
      "-ma lsass","--ma lsass","procdump",
      "esentutl /y /vss","vssadmin create shadow",
      "ntdsutil","ifm create full",
      "\\system_web\\","aspnet_client\\system_web"),
    true, false)
| where ExchangeContext or SilkTyphoonRecon                          // narrow to actor-relevant context
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildName   = FileName,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256, ExchangeContext, SilkTyphoonRecon, ReportId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — w3wp.exe → shell / recon / cred-theft LOLBin on Exchange (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
let _exec_lolbins = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe",
    "net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe",
    "ipconfig.exe","quser.exe","reg.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe",
    "procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","sc.exe",
    "wmic.exe","dsquery.exe","ldifde.exe","curl.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe",
    "esentutl.exe","vssadmin.exe","ntdsutil.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| extend ChildName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildName in (_exec_lolbins)
| where Account !endswith "$"
     or ChildName in ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","ntdsutil.exe","vssadmin.exe")
| extend SilkTyphoonRecon = iff(
    CommandLine has_any (
      "whoami /all",'net group "Domain Admins"','net group "Exchange',
      "nltest /dclist","nltest /domain_trusts",
      "Get-MailboxExportRequest","New-MailboxExportRequest",
      "-ma lsass","procdump",
      "esentutl /y /vss","vssadmin create shadow",
      "ntdsutil","ifm create full",
      "\\aspnet_client\\system_web"),
    true, false)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, ChildName,
          CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, SilkTyphoonRecon
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM - w3wp.exe Spawns Shell or LOLBin (Exchange Web-Shell Post-Exploitation)
id: 7c9b1f4a-2a3d-4e1f-9b6f-1f5a4d3e9c10
status: experimental
description: Detects IIS worker process w3wp.exe spawning interactive shells or recon/credential-theft LOLBins, the signature post-exploitation pattern of Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM, Operation Exchange Marauder) after dropping a China Chopper or ProxyLogon-class web shell into an Exchange virtual directory.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2025/03/05/silk-typhoon-targeting-it-supply-chain/
author: detection-eng
date: 2026/05/11
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0125
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\dsquery.exe'
      - '\ldifde.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\7za.exe'
      - '\esentutl.exe'
      - '\vssadmin.exe'
      - '\ntdsutil.exe'
  filter_legit:
    User|endswith: '$'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IIS application calling out to LOLBins (rare; investigate every hit)
  - SCOM / vendor management packs that exec inside the IIS worker (allowlist by parent commandline app-pool)
level: high
ucs.rationale: w3wp.exe (the IIS application pool worker) almost never spawns interactive shells, recon LOLBins, or credential-theft tooling under normal application traffic — when it does it is invariably a web-shell operator running commands via an .aspx implant. This was THE signature observation of the original ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065) compromise that Microsoft attributed to HAFNIUM / Si

_field_issues.message: `Domain` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `AccountDomain`?
`Admins` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Exchange` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Get` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`MailboxExportRequest` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`New` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — ASP.NET web shell file drop in Exchange / IIS virtual directories
ucs.description: Catches the file-write half of the Silk Typhoon kill chain: an .aspx/.ashx/.asmx/.asp web shell being created or renamed into an Exchange or IIS virtual directory (aspnet_client\system_web, FrontEnd\HttpProxy, ClientAccess\Owa\Auth, etc.) by an IIS-context, Exchange-context, or interactive-shell process. The exact path family Microsoft documented for HAFNIUM's ProxyLogon web shells and that Silk Typhoon has continued to use against Exchange and other internet-facing servers.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as file_name values(Filesystem.process_name) as process_name values(Filesystem.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asp") (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\ClientAccess\\Owa\\Auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\ClientAccess\\ecp\\Auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V14\\ClientAccess\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.user Filesystem.action
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
| `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| search action IN ("created","modified","renamed") OR action="write"
| eval risk_object=dest, risk_object_type="system"
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — web-shell .aspx/.ashx drop into Exchange / IIS virtual directories
let _shell_exts = dynamic([".aspx",".ashx",".asmx",".asp"]);
let _exchange_paths = dynamic([
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\system_web\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ews\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Owa\Auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\ecp\Auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V14\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V14\ClientAccess\ecp\auth\"]);
let _hafnium_filename_hints = dynamic([
    "supp0rt","OutlookEN","errorEE","errorEEE","errorEW","errorFF",
    "shell","healthcheck","web.aspx","aspnet_","discover",
    "RedirSuiteServerProxy","xx.aspx","one.aspx","help.aspx",
    "logon.aspx","default.aspx","current.aspx","HttpProxy","App_Web_"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| extend lFile = tolower(FileName), lPath = tolower(FolderPath)
| where _shell_exts has_any (lFile)                         // .aspx-family extension
     or FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx"
     or FileName endswith ".asmx" or FileName endswith ".asp"
| where lPath has_any (_exchange_paths)
| extend KnownHafniumName = iff(lFile has_any (_hafnium_filename_hints), true, false)
| extend WrittenByExchangeProc = iff(
    InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","umworkerprocess.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","rundll32.exe")
      or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\Exchange Server\"
      or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\inetsrv\",
    true, false)
| where WrittenByExchangeProc or KnownHafniumName             // suppress legitimate Exchange/CU installer writes via msiexec
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","setup.exe","updatecas.exe","servicing.exe","trustedinstaller.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, ActionType, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessAccountDomain,
          KnownHafniumName, WrittenByExchangeProc, ReportId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — web-shell .aspx drop into Exchange / IIS paths (ASIM ImFileEvent)
let _shell_exts = dynamic([".aspx",".ashx",".asmx",".asp"]);
let _exchange_paths = dynamic([
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Owa\Auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\ecp\Auth\",
    @"\Exchange Server\V14\ClientAccess\"]);
let _hafnium_filename_hints = dynamic([
    "supp0rt","OutlookEN","errorEE","errorEEE","errorEW","errorFF",
    "RedirSuiteServerProxy","shell","healthcheck","web.aspx",
    "one.aspx","xx.aspx","current.aspx","help.aspx","discover"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| extend lFile = tolower(TargetFileName), lPath = tolower(TargetFilePath)
| where TargetFileName endswith ".aspx" or TargetFileName endswith ".ashx"
     or TargetFileName endswith ".asmx" or TargetFileName endswith ".asp"
| where lPath has_any (_exchange_paths)
| extend KnownHafniumName = iff(lFile has_any (_hafnium_filename_hints), true, false)
| extend WrittenByExchangeProc = iff(
    ActingProcessName has_any ("w3wp.exe","umworkerprocess.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","rundll32.exe")
      or ActingProcessName has @"\Exchange Server\",
    true, false)
| where WrittenByExchangeProc or KnownHafniumName
| where ActingProcessName !has_any ("msiexec.exe","setup.exe","updatecas.exe","servicing.exe","trustedinstaller.exe")
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, EventType, TargetFileName, TargetFilePath,
          TargetFileSHA256, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine,
          ActorUsername, KnownHafniumName, WrittenByExchangeProc
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM - ASPX Web Shell File Drop in Exchange / IIS Virtual Directory
id: 9d1e4a72-8b03-4f6e-8a5c-2e1b6c0a7d11
status: experimental
description: Detects creation of an .aspx/.ashx/.asmx/.asp file inside an Exchange or IIS virtual directory used by HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon for web-shell deployment during ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065) and successor campaigns.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://www.volexity.com/blog/2021/03/02/active-exploitation-of-microsoft-exchange-zero-day-vulnerabilities/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2025/03/05/silk-typhoon-targeting-it-supply-chain/
author: detection-eng
date: 2026/05/11
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0125
  - attack.persistence
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_ext:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.aspx'
      - '.ashx'
      - '.asmx'
      - '.asp'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\'
      - '\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\'
      - '\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\Owa\Auth\'
      - '\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\ecp\Auth\'
      - '\Exchange Server\V14\ClientAccess\'
  filter_installer:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
      - '\setup.exe'
      - '\UpdateCas.exe'
      - '\Servicing.exe'
  condition: selection_ext and selection_path and not filter_installer
fields:
  - Image
  - TargetFilename
  - User
  - Hashes
falsepositives:
  - Exchange Cumulative Update / Security Update installation (filtered by Image)
  - Legitimate vendor add-on installations into Exchange path (rare; review every write)
level: high
ucs.rationale: The Exchange / IIS virtual-directory file paths in this detection are the exact families Microsoft, Volexity, and FireEye/Mandiant published in March 2021 as HAFNIUM ProxyLogon web-shell drop locations — specifically `inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\system_web\`, `Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\`, and the ECP auth subdirectory. Legitimate Exchange administration never writes new .as
